condition_code.description: Canonical condition codes: M, NM, LP, MP, HP, DMG. Default: NM. Human-readable aliases such as `near mint` and `lightly played` are accepted and normalized.
condition_code.title: Condition Code

finish.description: Accepted input values: normal, nonfoil, foil, etched. Canonical response values: normal, foil, etched. The input alias `nonfoil` is normalized to `normal`.
finish.title: Finish

lang.description: Catalog search language filter. Recommended codes include: en, ja, de, fr, it, es, pt, ru, ko, zhs, zht, ph. Search currently accepts the raw stored catalog language codes.
lang.title: Lang

language_code.description: Canonical language codes: en, ja, de, fr, it, es, pt, ru, ko, zhs, zht, ph. When omitted, the added inventory row inherits the resolved printing language. Common language-name aliases such as `english` and `japanese` are accepted and normalized.
language_code.title: Language Code

oracle_id.description: Card-level Oracle ID to resolve to one default printing by backend policy. When language is omitted, quick-add prefers English mainstream-paper printings in the default add scope before newer promo-like rows.
oracle_id.title: Oracle Id

condition_code.description: Canonical condition codes: M, NM, LP, MP, HP, DMG. Stored rows are normally normalized to these values.
condition_code.title: Condition Code

finish.description: Canonical finish values: normal, foil, etched.
finish.title: Finish

language_code.description: Canonical language codes: en, ja, de, fr, it, es, pt, ru, ko, zhs, zht, ph. Stored rows are normally normalized to these values.
language_code.title: Language Code

printing_selection_mode.description: How the concrete printing was chosen. `explicit` means the caller directly identified the printing. `defaulted` means backend resolution selected the printing.
printing_selection_mode.title: Printing Selection Mode

default_inventory.description: Optional default inventory slug when the CSV does not include an inventory column.
default_inventory.title: Default Inventory

dry_run.description: When true, validate and resolve the import using the real add-card workflow but roll back before commit.
dry_run.title: Dry Run

resolutions_json.description: Optional JSON array of explicit row resolutions for ambiguous CSV rows. Each item selects one suggested printing and finish for a specific csv_row.
resolutions_json.title: Resolutions Json

BulkInventoryItemMutationRequest.description: Specify exactly one bulk mutation operation per request. The current runtime supports add_tags, remove_tags, set_tags, clear_tags, set_quantity, set_notes, set_acquisition, set_finish, set_location, and set_condition.

acquisition_currency.description: Used by set_acquisition. Provide this field to set/replace acquisition_currency. Omit this field for every other bulk operation.
acquisition_currency.title: Acquisition Currency

acquisition_price.description: Used by set_acquisition. Provide this field to set/replace acquisition_price. Omit this field for every other bulk operation.
acquisition_price.title: Acquisition Price

clear_acquisition.description: Only applies to set_acquisition. When true, acquisition_price and acquisition_currency are cleared.
clear_acquisition.title: Clear Acquisition

clear_location.description: Only applies to set_location. When true, location must be omitted and location is cleared.
clear_location.title: Clear Location

clear_notes.description: Only applies to set_notes. When true, notes must be omitted and notes are cleared.
clear_notes.title: Clear Notes

condition_code.description: Used by set_condition. Canonical condition codes: M, NM, LP, MP, HP, DMG. Human-readable aliases such as `near mint` and `lightly played` are accepted and normalized. Omit this field for every other bulk operation.
condition_code.title: Condition Code

finish.description: Used by set_finish. Accepted input values: normal, nonfoil, foil, etched. Canonical response values: normal, foil, etched. The input alias `nonfoil` is normalized to `normal`. Omit this field for every other bulk operation.
finish.title: Finish

keep_acquisition.description: Only applies to merged set_location or set_condition changes. Choose whether the merged row keeps the target row or source row acquisition metadata.
keep_acquisition.title: Keep Acquisition

location.description: Used by set_location. Provide a location to set/replace location, or omit it when clear_location is true. Omit this field for every other bulk operation.
location.title: Location

merge.description: Only applies to set_location or set_condition. When true, a collision with an existing row is merged instead of returning a conflict.
merge.title: Merge

notes.description: Used by set_notes. Provide notes to set/replace notes, or omit it when clear_notes is true. Omit this field for every other bulk operation.
notes.title: Notes

quantity.description: Required for set_quantity. Omit this field for every other bulk operation.
quantity.title: Quantity

tags.description: Required for add_tags, remove_tags, and set_tags. Omit this field for clear_tags. Omit this field for non-tag bulk operations.
tags.title: Tags

BulkInventoryItemMutationRequest.title: BulkInventoryItemMutationRequest

available_languages.description: Catalog language codes available for the matched card. Common values include: en, ja, de, fr, it, es, pt, ru, ko, zhs, zht, ph.
available_languages.title: Available Languages

finishes.description: Canonical finish values: normal, foil, etched.
finishes.title: Finishes

is_default_add_choice.description: True when this printing matches the backend's current default quick-add choice for the same oracle_id. When omitted-finish quick-add would fail, every row is false.
is_default_add_choice.title: Is Default Add Choice

resolutions.description: Optional explicit row resolutions for ambiguous remote deck rows. Each item selects one suggested printing and finish for a specific source_position.

source_snapshot_token.description: Optional snapshot token returned by a prior dry-run deck URL import. When supplied, the backend reuses the short-lived signed normalized remote deck payload instead of refetching the provider.
source_snapshot_token.title: Source Snapshot Token

source_url.description: Public deck URL to import. V1 currently supports Archidekt, AetherHub, ManaBox, Moxfield, MTGGoldfish, MTGTop8, and TappedOut deck URLs.
source_url.title: Source Url

finish.description: Canonical finish values: normal, foil, etched.

InventoryDuplicateRequest.description: Create a new inventory and copy every source inventory row into it atomically. If duplication fails, the new inventory is not created.

target_description.description: Optional description for the duplicated inventory. When omitted, the source inventory description is copied.
target_description.title: Target Description

InventoryDuplicateRequest.title: InventoryDuplicateRequest

public_path.description: Browser-facing public share page path, for example `/shared/inventories/{share_token}`. In shared-service deployments, that page should fetch backend JSON through the proxied API route `/api/shared/inventories/{share_token}`; this field is not a proxy-aware API fetch URL.
public_path.title: Public Path

public_path.description: Browser-facing public share page path, for example `/shared/inventories/{share_token}`. In shared-service deployments, that page should fetch backend JSON through the proxied API route `/api/shared/inventories/{share_token}`; this field is not a proxy-aware API fetch URL.
public_path.title: Public Path

token.description: Reusable signed share token. Store/copy this only as part of the public share URL; it grants anonymous read-only access to this inventory's public share projection until rotated or revoked.
token.title: Token

InventoryTransferRequest.description: Transfer selected inventory rows, or the entire source inventory, into another inventory. The live mutation is atomic and all-or-nothing; if any requested row would fail, no rows are transferred. Use dry_run=true to preview copy, move, merge, or failure outcomes without mutating either inventory.

all_items.description: When true, transfer every row in the source inventory. Use either item_ids or all_items=true, not both.
all_items.title: All Items

dry_run.description: When true, return the planned transfer outcomes without mutating either inventory.
dry_run.title: Dry Run

keep_acquisition.description: Only applies when on_conflict is `merge`. Choose whether merged target rows keep the source row or target row acquisition metadata.
keep_acquisition.title: Keep Acquisition

mode.description: Transfer mode. Use `copy` to leave source rows in place or `move` to remove them.
mode.title: Mode

on_conflict.description: Conflict policy for target-inventory row identity collisions. Use `fail` to reject duplicate rows or `merge` to combine rows using the existing inventory merge rules.
on_conflict.title: On Conflict

InventoryTransferRequest.title: InventoryTransferRequest

keep_acquisition.title: Keep Acquisition

SetInventoryItemPrintingRequest.description: Change an existing owned row to a different printing of the same oracle card. Clients may also resubmit the current scryfall_id to confirm a defaulted row as explicit when finish and language stay unchanged. When finish is omitted, the backend keeps the current finish if the target printing supports it; otherwise it auto-selects the first supported finish in normal > foil > etched order.

finish.description: Optional explicit target finish. Accepted input values: normal, nonfoil, foil, etched. Canonical response values: normal, foil, etched. When omitted, the backend preserves the current finish if valid on the target printing; otherwise it auto-selects the first supported finish in normal > foil > etched order. Resubmitting the current scryfall_id is confirmation-only and cannot be used as a same-printing finish change.
finish.title: Finish

keep_acquisition.description: Only applies when merge is true for printing changes. Choose whether the merged row keeps the target row or source row acquisition metadata.
keep_acquisition.title: Keep Acquisition

merge.description: When true, a collision with an existing row identity after the printing change is merged instead of returning a conflict.
merge.title: Merge

SetInventoryItemPrintingRequest.title: SetInventoryItemPrintingRequest

parameters.description: Catalog search language filter. Recommended codes include: en, ja, de, fr, it, es, pt, ru, ko, zhs, zht, ph. Search currently accepts the raw stored catalog language codes. Omit this parameter to prefer English printings by default. Use `all` to include every available catalog language.
